payload.text:
**Building the New Tab**

I'm currently focused on the "Content Genius AI" tab, specifically the new component file. I'm exploring how best to integrate image analysis with Gemini to generate compelling ad copy. My goal is to create a seamless user experience, allowing for both image uploads and text-based input for product descriptions. I'm prioritizing efficient data handling and prompt optimization for effective copy generation.


**Connecting the Component**

I've integrated the new `ContentGeniusAI` component into the main `App.tsx` and added a new tab button for easy navigation. I'm now focusing on standardizing the API calls. While the provided code snippet has inline `fetch` calls, I'm adapting it to use the `@google/genai` SDK and leveraging `process.env.API_KEY` for API key management, ensuring consistency with the project's architecture. I'm also ensuring the API key is handled correctly.


**Removing Unnecessary Code**

I'm removing `GlobalStyle` from the component as Tailwind handles the styling. I'm focusing on integrating the `@google/genai` SDK and streamlining the `generateContent` function, aiming for clean code and compatibility with the project's existing structure and utilizing `process.env.API_KEY`. The core UI structure from the provided code is being preserved.


**Consolidating Gemini Integration**

I'm moving the Gemini API call into `geminiService.ts` for a cleaner architecture. My focus is now on integrating the `@google/genai` SDK directly into this service, leveraging `process.env.API_KEY` for key management and adopting the more concise model naming format provided. The deprecation warning reinforces the need for this shift, and I'll keep the component itself focused on UI and user interaction.


**Addressing API Key Integration**

I'm now fully immersed in fixing the API key integration for the ContentGeniusAI component. The provided code has a hardcoded, empty API key, which obviously won't work, so I'm moving toward a robust solution. I'm prioritizing the `@google/genai` SDK and `process.env.API_KEY` for seamless key injection. This will involve the creation of a `geminiService.ts` with a `generateAdCopy` function called by my new component. My focus will then shift to updating `App.tsx` to handle the new tab, button, and component rendering, as well as considering how to integrate my function into the project's existing structure and coding conventions. I will be doing this with a focus on simplicity, and I'll keep the new function self contained.


**Integrating the Image Processing**

I'm now integrating image processing capabilities and have settled on using `gemini-2.5-flash` model for both image and text handling, simplifying the multimodal approach. The focus is on implementing the `generateAdCopy` function in `geminiService.ts` to utilize the new image input. I'm adapting the UI to fit within the existing tab structure in `App.tsx`, focusing on concise container adjustments for a seamless look. I've begun to remove unnecessary styles and focus on the core functionality.


**Adapting the UI**

I'm now integrating the `ContentGeniusAI` component into `App.tsx` and focusing on a consistent UI. I'm removing the `min-h-screen` and background styles from the new component. I will add a tab button and render `<ContentGeniusAI />` using an appropriate icon. I'm adapting the new component to fit seamlessly within the existing `App.tsx` structure for a cohesive visual style.


**Updating the Component Integration**

I'm now focusing on integrating `ContentGeniusAI` into the main `App.tsx` file while ensuring consistent icon usage and removing any unnecessary `min-h-screen` styling to avoid forced scrollbars within the tab. I'm also ensuring the correct `lucide-react` icons are being imported. Next, I'll move to building out the `geminiService.ts` file, and then build the component.


**Implementing Ad Copy Generation**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the `geminiService.ts` file, and I've started the `generateAdCopy` function. I'm focusing on crafting a comprehensive prompt tailored for a Vietnamese audience. I'm leveraging the `gemini-pro` model for its text generation capabilities. I'm also planning to integrate image data if provided. Next up is adapting the component to work with my `generateAdCopy` and making the tab for my new component visible.


**Finalizing Prompt Engineering**

I'm now refining the prompt within `geminiService.ts`, aiming to generate highly effective ad copy. I've focused on integrating Vietnamese-specific requests with detailed instructions on formatting, length, and content style. I'm prioritizing direct generation of the ad copy, and excluding any extraneous analysis. My goal is for the ad copy to be concise, compelling, and ready for immediate use.


**Refining Ad Copy Generation**

I've significantly updated the prompt to yield direct, Vietnamese ad copy. Now, image handling works, and I'm refining the component to handle both image and text inputs. My current goal is to have the UI and API calls work together for a streamlined experience.


**Streamlining the Component**

I'm now fully immersed in updating `App.tsx` and removing any extraneous code. My next goal is to implement the API calls into the component and the removal of the API key, replacing it with `process.env.API_KEY` for key management and adopting the more concise model naming format provided. The deprecation warning reinforces the need for this shift. I also plan to clean up any remaining styling imports and update the import statements to include `ContentGeniusAI`.


**Optimizing UI Integration**

I'm now fully immersed in refining the component's structure to achieve a seamless integration within the existing application. I'm focusing on cleaning up extraneous styling and ensuring the background colors are visually consistent. I'm prioritizing building out the `geminiService.ts` file, and then building the component, ensuring it integrates with the overall app. Next, I plan to finalize the API call to generate ad copy.


**Adapting Component Export**

I'm making minor tweaks to the `ContentGeniusAI` component. The export statement will match others in the project for consistency. I'm focusing on adopting the project's existing types, specifically `ProcessedImage`, for a more streamlined integration, while keeping things simple and functional.

entries.description: Added generateAdCopy function for the new ContentGeniusAI feature.
New component for generating ad copy using Gemini.
Imported ContentGeniusAI, added a new tab for it, and rendered it in the main content area.

entries.description: Add generateAdCopy function for ad text generation with 500 char limit.

payload.text: Cho thêm công thức bán hàng cho review sản phẩm và chia sẻ trải nghiệm

**Developing New Formulas**

I've been working on integrating two new sales formulas into the "ContentGeniusAI" tab: "Product Review" and "Experience Sharing." The goal is to enhance the tool's capabilities. I'm currently updating the `components/ContentGeniusAI.tsx` file to accommodate these additions and ensure seamless functionality.


**Integrating New Formulas**

I'm currently focused on the `formulas` array within `components/ContentGeniusAI.tsx`. I've added "REVIEW" (Đánh giá chân thực) and "EXPERIENCE" (Chia sẻ trải nghiệm) formulas, and the descriptions are in Vietnamese as requested. Also, I've confirmed the 1500 character limit in the `geminiService.ts` file is current, and the user hasn't yet provided any updates on a preferred character limit.


**Revising Character Limits**

I'm now investigating the character limit. While the `geminiService.ts` file currently reflects 1500 characters, my focus is now on confirming this is intentional based on previous instructions. The user's earlier prompt mentions 500 characters for ad copy, so I need to clarify whether that limit should apply to the new formulas as well or be revised to match the current 1500. It seems the history has some conflicting information!


**Analyzing Current State**

I'm currently focused on the `geminiService.ts` file, where the 1500-character limit is clearly stated in Vietnamese on line 223. This confirms the existing limit is intentional, contradicting some earlier information and requests in the user history. I'll proceed with adding the formulas without modifying the limit at this time.


**Appending New Formulas**

I've added two new formulas to the `formulas` array within `components/ContentGeniusAI.tsx`. These are "REVIEW" (Đánh giá chi tiết ưu/nhược điểm) and "EXPERIENCE" (Trải nghiệm sử dụng thực tế), completing their respective entries with Vietnamese descriptions, as instructed. I've also included icons for each. There is no need to make any changes to the `geminiService.ts` file or character limits.


**Finalizing Component Updates**

I've confirmed that the `geminiService.ts` file dynamically integrates the formula and description in the prompt. Updating only the `ContentGeniusAI.tsx` component is sufficient. The verification confirms the correct handling of formula and description variables. My next task is to assess the styling updates needed to accomodate the additions in the UI.


**Adjusting the Layout**

I'm now revising the layout of the `ContentGeniusAI.tsx` component to accommodate the newly added formulas. While the current grid layout works, I'm exploring different options to ensure optimal display and user experience with the additional items. I'm focusing on ensuring visual balance and accessibility within the existing design framework. The current grid structure is `grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3` which can result in display gaps, so I will investigate potential adjustments to keep the UI clean.


**Optimizing Formula Names**

I've decided to refine the new formula names for clarity. I've updated "Review 1-1" to simply "Review" for consistency, and all seems good to go! I've also confirmed that the prompt logic in `geminiService.ts` correctly handles the content angle for reviews.


**Optimizing UI Layout**

I'm now revising the layout of the `ContentGeniusAI.tsx` component, but with a focus on 7 items. The existing `grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3` structure leaves gaps for 7 items. I'm exploring layout adjustments to maintain a clean and user-friendly display, potentially using dynamic sizing or different grid configurations. No further changes needed to formulas or service at this time.


**Refining Icon Choices**

I'm now making the final decisions on the icons for the new formulas within the `components/ContentGeniusAI.tsx` file. I'm choosing between various standard emoji options to best represent "Review" and "Experience." I've settled on "⭐" for Review and will use '🗣️' for Experience in the formulas array. I've also verified that the existing layout can accommodate the new number of items without significant UI changes. No updates required.


**Updating Emoji Icons**

I've replaced the existing icons in the formulas array. The "Review" formula now uses "⭐" and the "Experience" formula uses "🗣️", as decided. These new icons are now reflected in the UI. No further changes are required.

Tôi sẽ cập nhật thêm 2 công thức viết nội dung mới là **Review (Đánh giá)** và **Experience (Chia sẻ trải nghiệm)** vào bộ công cụ ContentGeniusAI của bạn.

Dưới đây là các thay đổi chi tiết:

entries.description: Thêm công thức Review và Chia sẻ trải nghiệm vào danh sách formulas
